  "weights": {

  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For this review, should the primary focus be (A) argument soundness and evidence-claim alignment (R1.1–R1.3), (B) writing polish at the sentence/paragraph level (R1.6), (C) section-level structure and flow (R1.5), or (D) positioning/novelty and scholarship (R1.4/R1.7)? You may pick one primary and one secondary.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Reconstruct the section’s core thesis and contribution claim to ensure the framing of GRADPHY and the residual-amplification prescription is coherent and well-scoped.",
    "R1.2": "Evaluate whether the gradient-norm diagnostics logically justify the stated training prescription and recommendations without internal contradictions.",
    "R1.3": "Check that the quantitative results described (e.g., Table 2 and related metrics) actually support the generalization and performance claims made in the text.",
    "R1.6": "Provide sentence- and paragraph-level suggestions to improve clarity, grammar, and concision while preserving technical meaning.",
    "RX.1": "Highlight where conclusions rest on limited evidence or extrapolation and recommend phrasing that communicates uncertainty appropriately.",
    "RX.2": "Guard against exaggerated or unsupported claims by ensuring the strength of language matches the demonstrated evidence.",
    "RX.3": "Constrain the review strictly to the provided section’s content without introducing new experiments or external literature.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid guidance that could mislead practitioners toward unsafe or invalid deployments by ensuring recommendations are responsibly framed.",
    "RX.5": "Anchor every observation and recommendation to specific statements or reported numbers in the provided text for traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For this review, should the primary focus be (A) argument soundness and evidence-claim alignment (R1.1–R1.3), (B) writing polish at the sentence/paragraph level (R1.6), (C) section-level structure and flow (R1.5), or (D) positioning/novelty and scholarship (R1.4/R1.7)? You may pick one primary and one secondary.

rationale.R1.1: Reconstruct the section’s core thesis and contribution claim to ensure the framing of GRADPHY and the residual-amplification prescription is coherent and well-scoped.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluate whether the gradient-norm diagnostics logically justify the stated training prescription and recommendations without internal contradictions.
rationale.R1.3: Check that the quantitative results described (e.g., Table 2 and related metrics) actually support the generalization and performance claims made in the text.
rationale.R1.6: Provide sentence- and paragraph-level suggestions to improve clarity, grammar, and concision while preserving technical meaning.
rationale.RX.1: Highlight where conclusions rest on limited evidence or extrapolation and recommend phrasing that communicates uncertainty appropriately.
rationale.RX.2: Guard against exaggerated or unsupported claims by ensuring the strength of language matches the demonstrated evidence.
rationale.RX.3: Constrain the review strictly to the provided section’s content without introducing new experiments or external literature.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid guidance that could mislead practitioners toward unsafe or invalid deployments by ensuring recommendations are responsibly framed.
rationale.RX.5: Anchor every observation and recommendation to specific statements or reported numbers in the provided text for traceability.
